publications.title: A Shared TCR Bias toward an Immunogenic EBV Epitope Dominates in HLA-B*07:02-Expressing Individuals.
publications.authors: Rowntree LC, Nguyen THO, Farenc C, Halim H, Hensen L, Rossjohn J, Kotsimbos TC, Purcell AW, Kedzierska K, Gras S, Mifsud NA.
publications.executive_summary: The study examined CD8+ T cell responses to a dominant EBV epitope (EBVRPP, EBNA-3 residues 379–387) presented by HLA-B*07:02, a highly frequent human HLA allele, using crystal structure determination of a representative TRBV4-1+ TCR in complex with the peptide–MHC. The researchers found that EBV-specific CD8+ T cell responses in HLA-B*07:02+ individuals display a striking shared TCR bias, with TRBV4-1 and TRAV38 gene usage predominating across five of six donors studied, indicating antigen-driven selection of TCRs despite typical clonal diversity. The TCR crystal structure revealed the molecular basis for this conserved TRBV4-1 usage and immunodominance of the EBVRPP epitope. This work is significant for understanding how persistent viral infections shape public TCR repertoires in common HLA-restricted populations and provides structural insight into the molecular features that drive TCR convergence toward a widely recognized viral epitope, with potential implications for vaccine design and TCR engineering approaches targeting EBV.
